title: The Absolute Best Prime Day Deals
gist: Wired's editorial team curates tested Prime Day deals across 7 product categories, noting that while Amazon's annual sale has degraded in quality (more fake deals, obscure brands), genuine discounts of 20–50% still exist on vetted products from Apple, Google, Anker, Ninja, and Dyson running June 23–26.

insights.claim: Amazon Prime Day's success has spawned widespread fake deals and influx of obscure brands, degrading the overall deal quality compared to earlier years.
insights.speaker: Louryn Strampe
insights.so_what: Amazon's deal event is maturing into noise; curated editorial filtering (Wired, Wirecutter) becomes a moat and traffic driver, while obscure brand ad spend on Amazon rises structurally.
insights.quote: Prime Day deals aren't what they once were—its success has inspired a massive number of fake deals and attracted obscure brands

insights.claim: The Apple Watch Series 11 reached $279 (30% off) on Amazon during Prime Day—the same discounted price as the Apple Watch SE 3—temporarily eliminating the entry-level SE's price advantage.
insights.speaker: Boutayna Chokrane
insights.so_what: Aggressive Prime Day discounting compresses Apple's own product-tier pricing ladder, potentially cannibalizing SE volumes and signaling Apple's tolerance for deeper discounts via retail partners.
insights.quote: Right now, the Series 11 is priced the same as the better value SE 3 (44 mm)—which is also on sale.

insights.claim: The MacBook Air (M5, 2026) dropped to $949 on Amazon, reinforcing its position as the dominant laptop recommendation even as Windows competitors like the HP OmniBook and Microsoft Surface offer premium alternatives at higher prices.
insights.speaker: Luke Larsen
insights.so_what: Apple's laptop ASP advantage is structural; Windows OEMs cannot undercut MacBook Air on value at this tier, pressuring margins for Microsoft Surface and HP premium lines.
insights.quote: As the price of its competitors continues to rise, the MacBook Air holds its spot even tighter as the best laptop in the world.
insights.locator: Best Apple and Apple Accessory Deals section

insights.claim: Portable power stations from Anker (Solix C1000 at $380–$400) and Jackery (Explorer 1000 at $400) are featured as mainstream Prime Day deals, reflecting consumer adoption of off-grid and backup energy products beyond the early-adopter segment.
insights.speaker: Simon Hill
insights.so_what: Portable power station category is reaching mass-market price points (~$400/kWh); watch Anker and Jackery unit volumes as leading indicators for consumer energy storage demand ahead of larger home battery adoption.

insights.claim: iRobot's Roomba Max 705 Combo listed at $899 (31% off) competes against Eufy's Omni C28 at $450 (44% off), illustrating a bifurcated robot vacuum market where AI docking-station combos command 2x premiums.
insights.speaker: Nena Farrell
insights.so_what: iRobot (now Amazon-owned) faces intense price competition from Chinese brands like Eufy at the $400–$500 tier; premium positioning requires genuine AI differentiation to justify the 2x price gap.
